name: generic-feedback-runtime
purpose: Own deterministic admission, replay, verdict-bound cuts, and dispatch intent for a generic feedback stream.

fit_evidence: The six-phase protocol has shared durable state authority.
Replay and idempotency require one transactional append authority.

boundary.owns: One ordered event chain and derived projection per stream
boundary.mechanism: Verify capabilities, decide pure transitions, atomically append canonical events, and replay
boundary.state_authority: SQLite is the sole durable append authority per database; BEGIN IMMEDIATE serializes writers

state_model: A seven-state linear FSM from detached through dispatched, validated with fsm-design.

protocol.versioning: Breaking envelope, cut, dispatch, or policy changes require a new schema or policy version.

invariants: Capabilities bind exact role and stream.
One principal cannot hold multiple stream roles in v1.
Causal parents and input cuts are exact.

runtime.ordering: Commands are serialized by BEGIN IMMEDIATE and sequence per stream.
runtime.concurrency: Independent databases may progress concurrently; competing writers to one database serialize.
runtime.backpressure: SQLite busy timeout bounds lock waiting and exposes failure to the caller.
runtime.cancellation: The six-event kernel has no cancellation command; outer-loop interruption is specified separately.
runtime.timeouts: Adapter and dispatch waits are outside the reducer and bounded by the loop contract.

durability.persistence: Append canonical event bytes before returning success.
durability.atomic_commit: Resolve request idempotency, verify the full stream, and append one event in one immediate transaction.
durability.recovery: Reopen, verify every row and full fold, then derive the replay projection.
durability.effect_idempotency: Ports receive the request id as an adapter idempotency key and a pinned cut.
durability.migration: No automatic migration in v1; incompatible schemas fail closed.

failure_model: Tampered canonical bytes, SQL columns, request hash, or chain fail closed.
Process restart and transaction rollback are supported; power-loss proof is not claimed.

traces: Event envelopes carry request, sequence, causation, principal, cut, and hashes.
metrics: Append conflicts, integrity failures, phase rejections, and replay duration are recommended outer-shell metrics.
observability.redaction: Capability secrets and signatures are never serialized into the event log.

security: Authority derives only from verified out-of-band capabilities.
Payload actor strings are untrusted data.
verification: Table-test phase, role, cut, and parent guards.
falsifiers: If durable state, shared replay, and idempotency disappear, collapse the kernel into a pure module.
